context: Tests whether the agent structures the spec file following RSpec conventions: describe for class/method, context for scenarios, spec path mirroring source path, prefer let over let!, and no 'and' in example descriptions. Also verifies frozen_string_literal pragma and subject definition.

checklist.name: Spec file at mirrored path
checklist.description: The spec file is placed at spec/services/notifications/user_notifier_spec.rb — mirroring the source path app/services/notifications/user_notifier.rb

checklist.name: RSpec.describe uses module::class
checklist.description: The top-level RSpec.describe references the full constant Notifications::UserNotifier (or described_class equivalent)

checklist.name: describe for method
checklist.description: A describe block is used for the '.call' class method (e.g. describe '.call')

checklist.name: context for scenario variations
checklist.description: At least two context blocks are used to describe scenario variations — phrased with 'when' or 'with' (e.g. 'when event_type is invalid')

checklist.name: let used for test data
checklist.description: Test data (user_id, event_type, params) is set up using let blocks, not bare instance variables or local variables in before hooks

checklist.name: No let! for non-setup data
checklist.description: let! is NOT used for data that does not need to exist unconditionally — only plain let is used for parameters and simple values

checklist.name: No 'and' in example descriptions
checklist.description: No it block description contains ' and ' — each example tests exactly one behavior

checklist.name: Frozen string literal
checklist.description: The spec file begins with # frozen_string_literal: true

checklist.name: Subject defined
checklist.description: A subject or named subject is defined for the main service call (e.g. subject(:result) { described_class.call(...) })

checklist.name: let_it_be NOT used
checklist.description: let_it_be is NOT used anywhere in the spec file (test-prof is not in this project)
